resources.string: Action recorded (will be taken at next cycle).
evolIA stopped — messaging inactive (messages are erased on stop).
Set up owner auth first: press Start Evolia.
Share this address — peers transfer BTC-e to it and your balance updates automatically on-chain:\n\n%1$s

Your BTC-e is the value evolIA builds from your activity. \"On-chain\" means a transfer is written on a blockchain — a shared, tamper-proof ledger — so it cannot be faked or spent twice. Exchanging on-chain needs three things, explained below: a connection to the blockchain (RPC), a tiny network fee (gas), and the recipient\'s address. New to crypto? Read on — each step is explained plainly.
1. What is an RPC, and how to set it

A blockchain runs on many computers called nodes. An RPC is simply the web address (a URL) of one such node — the door the app knocks on to read balances and send transfers. You also give a chain_id, a number that identifies which network you're on.

Where to get one:
• To try it with friends, run a free local node (e.g. Ganache) on a PC, or use a free public test network — no money involved.
• Free node providers also hand you a ready RPC URL.

Where evolIA reads it: the file evolia_chain_config.json (rpc_url and chain_id). If it's empty or missing, evolIA stays in LOCAL mode and nothing is settled on-chain. Tip: start on a test network — it behaves exactly the same, with zero risk.
2. What \"gas\" is, and how to fund it

Every blockchain charges a small fee, called gas, to process a transaction. Gas is paid in the network's own coin (its "native coin"). You only ever need a tiny amount.

Two situations:
• Test network (recommended to start): gas is free. Local accounts come pre-funded, or a free "faucet" hands you test coins. No bank, no card, no money.
• Real public network: the native coin costs real money, so you buy a small amount and send it to your address (shown at the bottom of this page).

How to get the coin with real money: open an account on an exchange such as Binance or OKX, then top it up — in Madagascar this is commonly done with Mobile Money (MVola, Orange Money, Airtel Money) through the exchange's P2P market, or with a Visa/Mastercard card, or a bank transfer. Buy a small amount of the network's native coin, then withdraw it to your evolIA address. IMPORTANT: pick the matching network when withdrawing — sending on the wrong network loses the funds.
3. Sending, receiving, and using Binance / OKX

Good to know: BTC-e lives inside evolIA's contract and moves directly between evolIA users. It is NOT a coin listed on Binance or OKX — you cannot send BTC-e to an exchange. Exchanges are only used to obtain the gas coin that funds your address.

To send: ask the other person for their evolIA address (their Receive screen shows a 0x… address). Tap Transfer, paste the address and the amount, and confirm with your PIN/password. The blockchain settles it and it can never overspend.

To receive: tap Receive and share your 0x… address. When someone transfers to it, your balance updates on its own — receiving is automatic, nothing to do.

If you use Binance/OKX: that's where you withdraw the gas coin to your evolIA address (a standard 0x… address, on the matching network). The BTC-e exchange itself still happens between evolIA users, inside the app.

• Your private key stays encrypted on this phone — never share it, your PIN, or your password.
• Always double-check the address and the network before sending. Transfers are final.
• Begin on a test network with people you trust: same experience, no money at risk.
• Keep only a little gas — fees are tiny.
For peer messaging, turn on Bluetooth and Wi-Fi so your messages can reach nearby peers. Bluetooth works with no internet.
For offline peer messaging, turn on Bluetooth so your messages can reach nearby peers without internet.
Turn on Wi-Fi so your messages can reach peers on your local network.
To keep accumulating value in the background, allow evolIA to run without battery restrictions. It keeps running until you press Stop.
Move and orient your phone — the gyroscope, magnetic field, steps, gravity and altitude all feed your value while evolIA runs.
